entry.title: Re: How to create an XML structure of header and items in...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I suppose this is the information you're looking for?

RAP Data Services for Print Forms

In addition, I asked our internal chatbot for support. Here's the response:

To create an XML structure of header and items in RAP Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you need to ensure that both the header and item entities are correctly defined and linked in your CDS views and behavior definitions. Here is a step-by-step guide:

Step 1: Define CDS Views 

  1. Header Entity: Define a CDS view for the header entity. This should include all necessary fields for the header.

    define root view entity ZRAP_Header
as select from your_header_table
{
  key id as HeaderID,
  field1,
  field2,
  ...
}

  2. Item Entity: Define a CDS view for the item entity. This should include a foreign key to the header entity.

    define view entity ZRAP_Item
as select from your_item_table
{
  key id as ItemID,
  header_id as HeaderID,  // Foreign key to header
  item_field1,
  item_field2,
  ...
}

Step 2: Define Behavior

  1. Header Behavior: Define the behavior for the header entity, including the association to the item entity.

    define behavior for ZRAP_Header alias Header
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
  association [0..*] to ZRAP_Item as _Items on _Items.HeaderID = HeaderID;
}

  2. Item Behavior: Define the behavior for the item entity.

    define behavior for ZRAP_Item alias Item
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
}

Step 3: Service Definition and Binding

  1. Service Definition: Expose the header and item entities in a service definition.

    define service ZRAP_Service
{
  expose ZRAP_Header as Headers;
  expose ZRAP_Item as Items;
}

  2. Service Binding: Create a service binding to expose the service as an OData service.

Step 4: Test the Application

  • Use the Fiori Elements app to test the application. Ensure that the UI annotations are correctly set to display both header and item data.

By following these steps, you should be able to create a form that displays both header and item data in your RAP application.

Additional information: ABAP Restful Application Programming Model (RAP) - SAP Community

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Calibration Dashboard View – Configuration Options
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
 
I am currently working with Calibration Templates in SSFF and I need clarification regarding the Dashboard View configuration. My issue is specifically related to the Dashboard View, not the Matrix Grid View.
 
When I access the Dashboard section within the template configuration, the area appears visible but completely non-editable. I am not able to modify the order of the charts, edit what is displayed, or adjust any configuration settings. The section appears essentially read-only.
However, in several SAP Help pages and learning materials, I have seen examples of the Calibration Dashboard displayed with different visual styles and color schemes. In our instance, the dashboard uses strong, intense colors, whereas in SAP documentation examples, the dashboards appear in lighter or softer tones.
 
This brings us my main concerns: the dashboard displays (including the order of the evaluated attributes/ratings shown in the charts) are not fully consistent with what we would expect; and I cannot find any configuration options to adjust layout, order, or visual appearance.
 
Based on what I have been reading in the documentation, I would like to confirm:
1- Is the Calibration Dashboard View fully system-generated and non-configurable?
2- Is there any way to configure the charts, their order, or visual properties such as colors and layout?
3- Is this controlled via XML in the template, theme/branding settings, or any separate configuration tool?
 
These are mainly related to user experience and internal compliance requirements.
 
I am including some reference links below as examples of the differences in color and visual presentation that I have observed in SAP documentation and learning materials: 
Configuración de vistas de plantilla de calibración
Performing Calibration with SAP SuccessFactors Performance Manage
 
Any clarification or official documentation reference would be greatly appreciated.
 
Thank you in advance!
 
SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: Accruals on subscription licenses
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

I asked an internal chatbot for support. The following information sources were used:

Accrual and Deferral Posting with Purchase Order in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

Accruals Management - Purchase Order Accruals and Deferrals

Here's the response:

To address your requirement of deferring and recognizing purchase costs over the subscription term using the same recognition logic as revenue, you can consider the following approach:

  1. Purchase Order Creation:

    • Create purchase orders in S/4HANA for the software licenses you resell. This will allow you to perform a 3-way match with the supplier invoices and link the costs to the revenue for margin reporting.

  2. Accrual and Deferral Management:

    • Utilize the Accruals Management functionality in S/4HANA  to automate the calculation and posting of accruals and deferrals. This can be configured to match the revenue recognition method you are using, ensuring consistent margins across periods.
    • The system can calculate accruals and deferrals based on planned costs in the purchase order, allowing for a linear distribution of costs over the subscription period. This is similar to the revenue recognition process you described.

  3. Matching Revenue and Cost Recognition:

    • Ensure that the deferral method for costs matches the revenue recognition method by configuring the accrual engine to use the same logic. This can be done by setting up the accrual item types and methods to align with your revenue recognition setup.

  4. Integration with Subscription Billing:

    • If you are using SAP Subscription Billing, ensure that the integration with S/4HANA is set up to facilitate the seamless flow of data between the systems. This will help in aligning the cost and revenue recognition processes.

By following these steps, you can achieve a consistent and accurate reflection of margins across periods, aligning the cost recognition with the revenue recognition logic you are using.

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Naming/Set up company code ID
entry.content:
Dear @marcel_f,

Point 1. There was no hard switch that makes numeric company codes invalid

In Public Cloud the company code key is still 4-character alphanumeric 4-digit alphanumeric ID in the help text. That means purely numeric IDs like 1010 are still valid

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/22fc75c2e32b429f8791940933d3961e.html

Point 2. What did change is mainly the recommended / guided naming convention in CBC

The latest CBC guide explains that SAP uses country/region codes and IDs to create country/region-specific values and that a value can consist of:

  • the country/region code or ID alone or
  • the code/ID plus additional characters

For example, the guide’s country/region table shows Germany = code DE, country/region ID 10, so 10DE is simply 10 (country/region ID) + DE (country code), which matches the convention

Also, in the Set Up Organizational Structure procedure, the guide explicitly instructs for the company code:

  • ID: <Country/Region ID> and in practice you then complete it to 4 chars with additional characters as needed

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

Point 3. Why you saw Starter vs Customer differences

This is typically not because numeric is forbidden but because:

  • Starter/evaluation systems often have pre-delivered demo IDs so 1010 may already exist pushing you to pick another ID like 10DE and/or
  • guided CBC flows steer you toward the country/region ID-based pattern

Summary:

  • Both 1010 and 10DE can still be possible in Public Cloud as long as the ID is 4 characters and not already used
  • SAP current guidance is to align new org-unit IDs to country/region ID + optional extra characters which is why you increasingly see 10DE-style IDs
  • The note you attached is not strictly for the Public Cloud version

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP CDC / Gigya - How to remove phoneNumber field using a REST API call?

Is there any REST API that can be used to remove the phoneNumber field?

entry.title: Cash Flow Report in SAP Business One
entry.content:
The Cash Flow report is one of the key reports you can find among the financial reports in SAP Business One. However, there is not much information on how to use this report for your key business solutions.

First at all – why is it so important?

The Cash Flow report lets you see and foresee your liquidity balance for a certain period of time with different security levels1 from cash accounts to Business Partners forecast. The following diagram represents the security level of different components that affect your liquidity from the point of view of SAP Business One: 

Let’s see how this information is displayed in the report in SAP Business One2.: 

Like all other reports, the Cash Flow report requires you to enter the time frame to collect data. So, this is the first mandatory step. In this example, I run the report in the middle of the period, so I can see the real data for February and future data for March.

With weekly intervals, I can see the following results:

There are 9 collapsed lines for every 7 days starting from February 1. In the report header you can select or deselect different security levels. The effect of that can be seen in the results. Here you can see the difference once I deselect customer liabilities: 

Customer liabilities are taken away as a liquidity source and as a result there is cash shortage by the end of February. Let’s take a closer look at the customer liabilities now by expanding the relevant week and security level to understand why it is at the end of February and not earlier or later.  

The Invoice 1064 was issued in January with total amount 2,120.00 $. However, in the report it is displayed only in February and only a half of the total amount. This is because amount 1,060.00 $ is taken from the installments table of this Invoice: 

Let’s check how the data changes in case you have some statistics about business partner payment experience and you want to consider that in your report. So, this business partner has this information already in his master data: 

 

And going back to the selection criteria you can add one more criterion: 

 

And see results: the customer liabilities are now moved one week later – 

You can also run the report only for specific business partners in case it is required. For that you can update your selection criteria on the Business Partner tab: 

In this way, SAP Business One will check only the documents that are issued to the business partners you have selected.

Let’s check another security level: cash accounts. First of all, we can select or deselect specific accounts on the selection criteria Cash tab: 

SAP Business One will list here all the accounts for which you have selected Cash Account in their master data:

And secondly, like for the marketing documents SAP Business One operates with the due date of the transaction when running the Cash Flow report, the same happens for the transactions booked to the cash accounts:

Although the payment was made in February, you will be able to use the money only in March. This can be a result of the deferred bank transfers. The due date of the credit card voucher or check is also taken into consideration for the payments with checks or credit cards. In the same way as the customer liabilities, you can deselect this security level in the document header to check what the results will be.

To see how the customer or vendor forecast affects the Cash Flow report, you need to add to your selection criteria anything or all from the following selection criteria: blanket agreements, document drafts, recurring transactions, or marketing documents that are not posted to the business partner account. However, it can affect your cash flow.

Let’s take a look how these documents will affect the report:

Delivery No. 1182 makes changes to the cash balance with date 03/03/26. The date is calculated, according to the payment terms, to be 03/02/26:

Now, after discussing the security levels, let’s continue with the other features of this report.

Let’s start with the first line OB; there’s no value there. The OB is used in SAP Business One as opening balance. And the cash flow allows you to enter the value:

or SAP Business One will calculate that for you:

The Add Closing Balances checkbox is aplicable for the localizations where you close the balance sheets accounts during the year-end transactions. I recommend having it selected there.

Now when I run the report the situation dramatically changes:

You can easily check, by expanding and using navigation arrows, how the data got to the report:

In this case I would recommended to check if some transactions are not booked.

You can also add some transactions that are in your mind, but you do not have any recurring postings for that or journal vouchers. For example, you are going on a business trip, and you plan some business dinners with partners. You can enter the amount to the Include Projected Postings table as below:

And see the results under the Cash Accounts security level (this security level is defined by you in the table above):

In case these expenses or income are regular, you can also define recurrence periods. Any line you enter in this table is saved, so you do not need to enter the same information every time you run the report.

With that I want to finish my story about SAP Business One Cash Flow report, and I wish you good luck in your further discovery of the Cash Flow features. I would recommend checking system Crystal Reports layouts, 😉.

And one more thing: I would like to invite you to provide feedback or ask clarifying questions regarding the topic in the comments section. It is also possible to submit the improvement requests for this feature (and others as well!) on the Customer Influence site: https://influence.sap.com/SAPBusinessOne, and you can also visit the Implementation Arena for useful implementation tips.

For more information, visit SAP Business One training: Generating the Cash Flow, Aging and Dunning Letters Reports

---------------------------------------------------------

  1. A security level is the level of probability that the transaction will turn to cash (incoming and outgoing).
  2. The screenshots are taken from the demo database OEC Computers with the US localization setup.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM Production Process Design Read Indicator Dummy...
entry.content:
Hi @aziz_khan_,

I’m assuming that you have successfully completed the integration between SAP DM, SAP CC, and the SAP Production Connector.

My suggestion is: if you are familiar with Node.js or with programming in general, I recommend running a Node.js OPC UA server using the following project:
https://github.com/node-opcua/node-opcua
It’s very easy to set up and does not require advanced programming skills. You simply need to install it and run the server— it already includes a dummy subset of tags that are ready for read/write operations.

With this setup, you can connect your own server to SAP DM and experiment with tag read/write using SAP DM PPD.

Marco

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to query dimension and measure metadat...
entry.content:
Hello @christoffer-ebbesen ,

I think we need a bit more information to be able to help you. 

Is there a way to query metadata on measures and dimensions in SAP datasphere? Specifically the business/technical names of the measures and dimensions, and what view they belong to. 

For simple Business / Technical names, then yes - you can use the /$metadata endpoint to find all that.
Note: It returns an XML-file, and not a JSON file. This is mainly because SAP wants to see you suffer. Anyway.

In the metadata, you're find the technical name and the data type:

 

If you are asking for the lineage of a field, then sadly no. 
I gave a more detailed answer here. 

 

Right now i'm extracting this data into snowflake through the OData relational API, just to reimport it back into datasphere. It seems stupid to extract data from datasphere just to import back into the same system. 

Yes, that seems like a god-awful waste of time. Quick question though - Why? 
Are you doing transformations in snowflake, renaming columns?

entry.title: how to merge several companion contents recorded with different product versions into one content?
entry.content:
dear community,

The issue is that the Product version changes frequently (SAC is a public cloud application)
 
When Key Users are willing to edit content, they can't find it because it is Product version specific, and companion then records a new project content.
   
2 questions:
- how to merge several contents recorded with different product versions into one content?
- how, in the future, to get rid of the product version dependency?
 
thanks for your help,
François

entry.title: Quantity Field is Blank for Journal Entry Type RE
entry.content:
In the general ledger line item display for RE-type documents, the quantity field is missing for the GR/IR (goods receipt/invoice receipt) clearing account.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Quantity Field is Blank for Journal Entry Type RE
entry.content:
If there is a valuated goods receipt, the quantity is posted in the financial document for the goods receipt. It is the standard behavior that the quantity is not posted in the financial document of the invoice after a valuated goods receipt. This behavior avoids duplicate quantities in follow up processes and reports.

The system behaves as designed. Please use the 'Display Line Item Entry' app instead, as this app is based on backend table BSEG whereas 'Display Line Items in General Ledger' is based on backend table ACDOCA.

Please refer to the below KBA for more information

KBA - ##3062983 - Quantity Field is Blank for Journal Entry Type RE

entry.title: Permit to work in SAP Cloud ERP Private: Aligning safety and maintenance in real operations
entry.content:
How can maintenance teams stay both safe and efficient while handling increasingly complex assets and processes?

This question opened our latest Expert Talk, where Maria Silipo welcomed SAP experts Jörg Medack and Yakub Shah, from product management, together with guest speaker Marc Janssen from the company Vattenfall. The session focused on how the Permit to Work process in SAP Cloud ERP Private supports operational safety and strengthens maintenance execution.

Watch the full Expert Talk episode below:

As explained by the SAP experts, a permit to work is much more than a formality. It acts as an operational safety barrier, setting clear conditions for safe execution and reducing the likelihood of incidents. SAP has been modernizing this process since 2020, simplifying how planning, preparation, approvals, safety implementation and closure are handled across maintenance activities. When these two worlds come together, plants gain a digital, structured way to guide safe work from start to finish. 

Marc then provided the customer perspective. Vattenfall is one of Europe’s largest producers and retailers of electricity and heat, operating wind, biomass, gas, solar and district heating assets across multiple countries. With more than 20,000 employees and approximately 50,000 permits issued each year, the company relies heavily on a consistent and dependable safety process.

According to Marc, since implementing the SAP solution, Vattenfall has seen work becoming safer across the business units using it, measured by a significant drop in safety‑related incidents.

To support their internal policy that every maintenance job requires a permit, Vattenfall added a small extension. If a permit starts from an operation rather than the whole order, operation‑level data is transferred into the permit. Otherwise, the system uses the standard approach and copies information from the order header. This ensures that each permit always reflects the correct operational context.

Yakub then demonstrated how SAP’s integrated process works in practice. One of the design principles is to avoid repetitive work. When a user identifies a maintenance order that requires a permit, they can create it directly. Instead of starting from a blank form, the system encourages the use of templates linked to the technical object. These templates store information such as known hazards, typical mitigation measures, safety precautions, safety gates and required certificates. With this approach, operational data is taken from the order while safety‑critical data comes from the template, helping ensure that important aspects are not overlooked.

Some of the steps in creating and preparing a permit include:

  • Selecting the relevant permit type based on the job
  • Applying a template for the equipment or location
  • Copying operational details from the order
  • Automatically populating safety‑related details from the template
  • Defining whether the permit applies to the entire order or specific operations

Once created, the permit follows a clear approval flow. The solution supports the four‑eye principle through a mix of mandatory and optional approvals. Customers can configure hierarchical approvals as well. For example, a second mandatory approval may only become available after the first one is completed. Every approval, comment and status change is logged, giving full clarity on decisions.

One of the strongest points in the demo was the validation logic before a permit can be issued. The system checks whether required safety precautions have been marked as implemented and whether dependent objects, such as safety certificates, are properly reviewed and approved. If something is missing, the user is blocked from issuing the permit until the gap is resolved.

These checks can be placed before printing or issuing, depending on the organization’s process. In real plant environments, especially when using tablets, each action is logged with date, time and user, enabling a transparent and trustworthy audit trail. For companies with unique validation needs, the extensibility framework introduced in 2023 allows custom rules to be added on top.

 Check the complete demo in the video, starting on 03:31: https://youtu.be/N8sD04FwrIc?si=cSrutg_439G4bPz5

Practical operations also require flexibility. Permits may need to be suspended when a shift ends, revalidated after a break in work, or handed over to another technician. SAP supports all of these steps while keeping the full history. To help plant managers keep track, the standard dashboard provides KPIs such as active permits, suspended permits and overdue items. Vattenfall is exploring adding extra tiles to show maintenance orders with open permits, reflecting how their internal processes begin from the order.

Looking ahead, Marc shared two important wishes. First, a deeper integration of Permit to Work within SAP’s nine-phase maintenance model, so the system can prevent execution when a required permit is not approved. Second, the continued evolution of isolation management, which SAP is actively developing. Marc highlighted the need not only for the functional capabilities but also for extensibility, so customers can adapt the solution to their specific safety models.

Yakub and Jörg closed the session by summarizing what SAP has delivered recently and what is coming next. Permit to work is now fully Fiori-based, with extensible objects for work permits and safety certificates. Integration with maintenance orders will be delivered with the 2025 FPS1 release, and isolation management will also be part of that delivery. Additional capabilities, such as shared tagging, adjustments, and mass processing, are on the roadmap. On the maintenance side, SAP is working on making the phase model more flexible, giving customers more control over process insights.

The discussion made it clear that Permit to work in SAP Cloud ERP Private is evolving quickly, driven by customer scenarios and real operational needs. For companies like Vattenfall, the solution already brings measurable improvements in safety, reliability and transparency, and the roadmap promises even more integration and flexibility in the future.

Do you want to learn more? Continue the conversation with professionals in the SAP Community: SAP S/4HANA Asset Management | SAP Community

Check our complete playlist for all SAP Expert Talks and subscribe to SAP's channel on YouTube.

 Find all our assets
We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: Mass TECO for WBS Elements in SAP PS – Best Practice Options?
entry.content:
Hi all,

In SAP PS, we currently set TECO on WBS elements manually in Project Builder (CJ20N), one at a time:

  • Open Project Builder
  • Open the project
  • Select the target WBS element
  • Switch to Edit mode
  • Edit → Status → Complete Technically → Set
  • Save

Functionally this works as intended, but it becomes very time‑consuming at scale.

I’m looking to understand the best‑practice or commonly accepted approaches for mass TECO at the WBS‑element level, while preserving standard SAP controls and auditability (i.e., not bypassing validations).

Specifically:

  • Is CNMASSSTATUS / RCNMASSSTATUS considered the standard SAP tool for mass‑setting system status TECO on WBS elements? If so, are there known limitations or side effects (e.g., user status behavior, selection scope, reversibility)?
  • If CNMASSSTATUS is not available or appropriate, is the typical approach to use BAPI_BUS2054_SET_STATUS within a controlled program or background job (e.g., via BAPI_PS_INITIALIZATION → BAPI_BUS2054_SET_STATUS → BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT → COMMIT)? Any key gotchas to be aware of?
  • Finally, if multiple WBS elements exist under the same project definition, is there a reliable Project Builder / WBS overview method to multi‑select and TECO them together (as opposed to true mass processing across projects)?

Goal is simply to reduce manual effort while achieving the same system outcome and control posture as the standard Project Builder process.

Any real‑world patterns or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to implement method needs_confirmation
entry.content:
Hi @stefan_bogesch ,

Each UIBB can trigger a confirmation pop-up. Unfortunetly as you figured out, for MDG-MM, super classes of feeder classes configured some interfaces as final. For that, you can't use those UIBB's feeder classes for confirmation pop-up.

Therefore, I suggest you to use the Change Request UIBB for handling confirmation pop-up for materials if you don't have a suitable other custom UIBB. You can follow similar steps in this link because CR UIBB also has IF_FPM_GUIBB_FORM_EXT.

Be sure to use strict conditions when you create a reference for EO_CONFIRMATION_REQUEST because CR UIBB is common for other Data Models.

entry.title: Re: Need Clarification on Copying Production Transaction...
entry.content:
Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

Based on the details you provided, your understanding is largely correct. There is a clarification to be made for Public Cloud.

1) Limitation – Is our understanding correct?

Yes, in Public Cloud, the classic on premise style client copy or system refresh is not supported by customers. That is one of the limitations in Public Cloud. It is also correct that configuration and extensibility artifacts move through the Public Cloud lifecycle process, rather than through classic system copy methods. See Data Migration for more details.

An exception here is, the Public Cloud has Test Data Refresh, which can transfer data from Prod to Quality system. However, this is subject to your subscription, prerequisites and service scope. See the following reference for more insights. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/70a3253a3beb411c8fe626ec504ec436.html?locale=en-US  and Blog: Test Data Refresh service request on SAP for Me.

2) Alternative Solution Approach

Question: Is this the recommended approach for test data preparation in Public Cloud, or are there any additional SAP-supported tools for this scenario?

Answer: Yes, your proposed approach is valid and commonly used, especially when Test Data Refresh is not available, not in scope or not yet planned. A slight refinement to your listed approach is:

  • Test Data Container is helpful for the test automation parameterisation and tests you can repeat, but it is not a general production transactional data copy mechanism.
  • Additionally, if the client specifically needs production like transactional test data in QAS, you can suggest TDR as the supported path.

3) Final Clarification for the Client

Question: Is there any supported method in Public Cloud to copy or synchronize production transactional data into QAS/DEV? Or is this completely restricted by design?

Answer: There is no supported customer executed classic client copy or system refresh from PRD to QAS/DEV in Public Cloud. TDR is supported to move data from Production to Quality, and it is subject to prerequisites and scope. For DEV and for scenarios outside TDR scope, it is recommended to use the Migration Cockpit and controlled dataset to test data creation.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
You have it: 

  • IF_SXML_READER/IF_SXML_WRITER with IF_SXML~CO_XT_JSON (used in XCO_JSON)
  • IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER (I think it is available from SAP_BASIS 8.16)

It will parse any JSON in the kernel. But getting it mapped into ABAP data is another story. The kernel does not know all ABAP types and data structures. And the creation of complex data structures shall be done in ABAP memory, but not in the kernel.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Business Rules: Understanding Modulo Function
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’ve decided to share some tips on hidden functions within SuccessFactors Business Rules that often go underutilized. Using these functions can help clean up your logic, reduce the number of complex 'If' conditions, and ultimately improve system performance.

Today, we’re focusing on the Modulo() function. Simply put, Modulo() returns the remainder of a number after division. For a deeper dive, check out the Implementing Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors document in the Help Portal.

For a better understanding I will cover two scenarios.

 

Scenario 1: Validating Full-Week Absences

In this case, we want to ensure an absence request covers exactly one or more full weeks. For example, in some organizations, employees are requested to take sabbatical in full week at a time. Here is how you can set this up:

 Image from Demo

 

In this logic, we check if the remainder of the total absence duration divided by 7 is anything other than zero. If the result is 0, the absence is exactly one or more full weeks. If the result is not 0, it indicates the employee is not taking the full week.

 

Scenario 2: Eligibility Every Four Years

In the beginning of my consulting days, I encountered a specific requirement: a company policy granting employees four weeks of sabbatical every four years. This means eligibility triggers at the 4th, 8th, and 12th years of seniority, and so on.

Rather than creating a separate 'If' condition for every possible seniority level, you can streamline the entire requirement using the Modulo() function:

Image from Demo

 

A remainder of zero indicates the seniority year is a multiple of four, confirming the employee’s eligibility for sabbatical. If the remainder is any other value, they are not yet eligible.

 

I hope this information has been helpful and encourages you to explore our standard functions to streamline your own implementation projects! As always, please discuss with your customers the pros and cons of adopting such a function in your projects.

entry.title: Preparing for SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release – Universal Full Profile View
entry.content:
With the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release, the Latest People Profile – Full Profile View will become universally available for all customers. As part of this change, the enablement status will shift from Customer Configured to Automatically On, meaning Full Profile View will be enabled by default and no longer controlled by a system setting.

While the Full Profile View will be turned on automatically, customers must still complete required configuration activities to ensure the experience is aligned with their business needs.

Action is required prior to the 1H 2026 universal push. Join this session to understand what preparation is required, what typically comes up as asks and use the chance to ask questions.

This event is limited to 1000 seats. If you cannot attend, the recording and presentation will be posted on the customer community blog: customer community blog.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud, on AVC Need to derive BOM quantity based on characteristic calculation
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Hello,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC).

Business requirement:
The finished product has a numeric characteristic Z_LONGITUD (unit: M).
This value should determine the component quantity of a cable material in the BOM (unit of measure: M), so that:

Configured Length → Component Quantity in BOM → Correct MRP Planning

What I have tried:

  • Set the BOM item category to Variable-Size Item (R)

  • Defined one size dimension (Length, unit M)

  • Created a formula key (e.g. ROMS1)

  • Attempted to assign ROMS1 = $ROOT.Z_LONGITUD in a procedure dependency → syntax error

  • Attempted to use constraint logic to influence quantity → not allowed

  • Variable-size item does not seem to allow direct mapping to a configurable characteristic in this tenant

Question:

Is it technically supported in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to dynamically derive the BOM component quantity from a configurable characteristic (numeric, continuous value)?

If yes:

  • What is the correct modeling approach?

  • Should this be done via Variable-Size Item reference characteristic?

  • Is Quantity Formula supported?

If not

  • It is side-by-side extensibility (BTP) required?

The goal is to ensure correct MRP planning based on the configured length.

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to hide "Compensation Structure" object for some...
entry.content:
Hi @rkumar1947 ,

If you're trying to hide the field during a hire or rehire process, please use rule scenario 'onInit Rules for Hire/Rehire'. Using an onInit rule for an existing employee will not work, as onInit rules apply only during Hire/Rehire scenarios. As an alternative, you could create a dedicated permission role for the HR team in that country(giving permission to this field) or configure an error message for other countries.

Regards,

VR

Hear directly from the experts who are cutting through the noise and delivering real results. Expect practical guidance, honest conversations, and frameworks you can put into action immediately.

These aren’t scripted presentations - they’re candid discussions about what’s working, what’s not, and what’s coming next.

Closing Takeaways

Wrap up with the key themes and next steps you can bring back to your team, including where to focus now to close the Engagement Divide.

 

💡Why This Event Matters

This isn’t another virtual conference filled with slides and buzzwords. It’s a global pulse check on how customer expectations are shifting — and how leading brands are adapting with speed, intelligence, and authenticity.

Attendees will walk away with:

If you’re serious about elevating your customer engagement strategy, this is the event that will shape your roadmap for the year ahead.

entry.title: Re: Change from MsgType FIDCC2 to ACC_DOCUMENT
entry.content:
Activities Required

Partner Profile Update (WE20)
Define outbound parameters for message type ACC_DOCUMENT with basic type ACC_DOCUMENT04. Remove or deactivate FIDCC2 outbound settings.

Distribution Model (BD64)
Adjust ALE distribution model to send ACC_DOCUMENT instead of FIDCC2. Assign the correct logical systems.

Process Code Assignment (WE42)
Ensure process code for ACC_DOCUMENT is active and points to the correct function module (ACC_DOCUMENT_POST).

Treasury System Outbound
Update the sending application (Treasury) to trigger ACC_DOCUMENT IDocs instead of FIDCC2. This usually means switching the message type in customizing or interface config.

Testing
Run end‑to‑end test: create posting in Treasury → IDoc generated → received in S/4 FI → posted successfully. Validate that no enrichment is required (segments map directly).

entry.title: Error F5428 Balance in 1 currencies during activity SLP in SIL for project settlement AuC
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are implementing a new tax ledger using the "Subsequent Implementation of a further Accounting Principle" tool (Release SAPK-10704INS4CORE).

During execution of activity SLP in SIL Cockpit we are facing error F5428 Balance in 1 currencies:

Balances have been found in 1 currencies with the following details:

Currency type Amount Currency
30
6.552,18-
EUR

The balance comes from a ledger group specific posting for project settlement against assets under construction and the missing amount comes from the line item posting to the AuC. The amount in that line is exactly 6.552,18 so it is as if the currency type 30 is missing completely only for that line item to balance the document out (so no FX issue).
There is also an equivalent posting in IFRS as the non-leading ledger in the system but this are not relevant for datatransfer in SIL)

Does anyone have any experience where this might come from?

High level scope of implementation of tax ledger:
1 company code on system
Source ledger = local GAAP (which is also leading ledger, IFRS is non-leading ledger)
One depreciation area in new tax ledger as source ledger only has one depreciation area posting in real time, rest are statistical

FIN General Ledger #Investment Management #SIL #SLP

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: Joule-Powered Migration, Datasphere Insights and How to find your community!
entry.content:
In this edition of SAP Community Voice, we bring you a mix of innovation, technical deep dives, and inspiring community stories. From AI-driven custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by Joule, to an in-depth exploration of SAP Datasphere's Object Store in Business Data Cloud, this week's highlights are packed with insights to help you stay ahead.

Beyond technology, we also celebrate the human side of our community, including an inspiring Ironman journey in our Hobbies Series, that proves resilience applies both in sports and in SAP projects. Plus, we have updates on the new CEI project for SAP Integration Suite, a reminder about the Ask Me Anything sessions with SAP Champions, and exciting event recaps and announcements from Southern California, Cyprus, and Philadelphia!

This edition is filled with practical insights, innovation, and global community energy. Let's dive in!

Featured Blog Posts

Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by Joule 

SAP expert Olga Dolinskaja, published a blog post about custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by Joule for Developers. Considering that the conversion system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is often a complex and time-consuming process. Olga, talks about how IT project managers face challenges defining scope, estimating effort, and understanding legacy code that often lacks documentation, while developers manually analyze ATC findings and interpret simplification notes. To address these challenges and accelerate migration projects, she explains how AI-based capabilities within SAP Joule for Developers now provide intelligent support for both managers and developers. Integrated into the Custom Code Migration app on SAP BTP ABAP environment and ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these capabilities offer Code Explain for understanding legacy logic and business purpose, ATC Explain for clear root-cause analysis of S/4HANA findings and much more. By reducing manual effort and providing contextual insights directly within development tools, these innovations help shorten migration timelines, enabling teams to meet deadlines and budget constraints more confidently. 

Read Olga blog post here 

Deep Dive into SAP Datasphere Object Store of BDC 

The HANA Data Lake Files (HDLF) service in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Datasphere provides a cost-effective and scalable way to store and process large data volumes as Delta tables. SAP Community member, Sreekanth Surampally published a blog post to explain how inside BDC, SAP manages the Foundation Services (FOS) object store for standard SAP data products, while customers manage the Datasphere HDLF object store for their own SAP and non-SAP data. Through the document, he runs the details of building on hyperscale object storage and powered by Apache Spark, plus how HDLF enables large-scale replication and transformation flows, supporting medallion architecture (Bronze-Silver-Gold), and offering the "SQL on Files" feature to query Delta tables directly from HANA Cloud without loading data into memory. With images to illustrate the architecture and configurations, this is a must read if you are looking for depth and clarity about the topic. 

Read Sreekanth blog post here 

Joule A2A: Connect Code Based Agents into Joule 

SAP expert Felix Bartler, published valuable content about how to connect code based agents into Joule. Starting off considering that many organizations have already built custom AI agents using frameworks such as LangChain or LangGraph, his post demonstrates how such a custom-built agent can be integrated into the Joule ecosystem using the new pro-code extensibility features together with the A2A (Agent2Agent) protocol. A minimal ReAct-based currency conversion agent built in Python with LangGraph is exposed via an A2A server and deployed on SAP BTP. Using Joule capabilities defined in YAML (scenarios and functions), the agent is connected through an HTTP destination in SAP BTP and invoked via the new agent-request action type. Felix shows that the A2A protocol enables secure, structured communication between Joule and remote agents, while the Joule CLI supports compilation and deployment of the capability into a standalone or productive assistant.  

Read Felix blog post here 

UI for the BTP Application Vulnerability Report 

SAP Mentor Wouter Lemaire created a very useful blog post about the SAP BTP Application Vulnerability Report, which is a tool that scans your deployed applications for known vulnerabilities and exposes the findings through an API. To make this feature even better, he created an open-source UI I on top of the BTP Application Vulnerability Report API, to enable a better user experience to amplify and simplify the research. Running through the details of each step of his project, his solution enables sorting, filtering, free-text search, severity-based visual indicators, and Excel export of findings, making it easier to prioritize remediation and share reports with stakeholders. The project requires no database, as all data is fetched in real time from the API, and he also shared that the full source code is available on GitHub for contributions and enhancements. Try it out and give your feedback! 

Read Wouter blog post here 

Community News 

Hobbies Series: Ironman 

In this week's Hobbies Series, we are excited to share the inspiring story of Vinit Kumar, Development Architect in SAP Cloud Lifecycle Management (CLM), who discovered the world of triathlons in an unexpected way. In June 2014, Vinit suffered a serious accident, which left him with a fractured leg, a metal plate, and unable to walk for more than 30 minutes for over a year. Determined to recover, he began running as therapy and embracing the mindset that "pain will kill the pain." In 2017, he completed a half marathon despite still having implants, and in May 2019, he set his sights on Ironman 70.3 Goa, even without prior experience. Combining swimming, cycling, and running, the challenge became what he describes as a “teacher” about discipline, resilience, and the belief that limits are often self-imposed. Despite additional surgery and many problems on the preparation in Goa, Vinit kept repeating his mantra: "Pain is temporary, finishing is permanent." And in November 2022, after 7.5 hours across a 1.9 km sea swim, 90 km bike ride followed by a run, he proudly crossed the finish line. He also shared how triathlon mirrors life at SAP in many ways, like process integration and performance analytics to systems thinking, resilience, and change management. Proving that whether implementing SAP solutions or training for a race, the principle remains the same: all it takes is all you have got. 

Read Vinit blog post here 

Register to the new CEI project for SAP Integration Suite in 2026 

This year, the Global Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) for SAP Integration Suite is back! SAP expert, Alex Bundschuh, shared on his latest blog post the details about the project on gathering early feedback on product improvements, planned enhancements, and new developments, ensuring they align with real business needs. Following last year's strong participation of over 180 customers and partners from more than 20 countries, for 2026 the discussions will cover key topics such as SAP PI/PO migration, AI and generative AI, developer experience, and much more. Registration is open from February 9 to March 13, 2026, with monthly feedback sessions starting in April and running through the end of the year. If you are interested in taking part, and help shaping the SAP Integration Suite product, make sure to register here (link leads to the customer influence page).  

Read Alex blog post here 

REMINDER: Ask Me Anything Sessions with SAP Champions  

Last edition we mentioned already about our SAP Champion Sebastiano Marchesini initiative Ask Me Anything, but we would like to remind everyone that this first upcoming session will be open for questions only until the end of this week (February 28). So, don´t miss out in the opportunity on participating on real discussions and getting to have your questions answered by an expert. Each month, a different SAP Champion with a specific area of expertise will answer both technical and non-technical questions, submitted openly or anonymously. For this first edition, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Communities YouTube channel. For more details, make sure to read Sebastino full blog post bellow. 

Read Sebastiano blog post here 

Events 

SAP Southern California meetup recap 

This month was super exciting for the SAP Southern California local community, because they had their first meetup. Hosted by SAP Champion Vijayeta Sharma, the event brought together an inspiring group of professionals looking forward to connecting, exchanging ideas, and strengthening regional collaboration. The strong energy and enthusiasm set up a positive tone for future gatherings, with plans already underway for more meetups, knowledge-sharing sessions, and community-driven discussions. Participants are encouraged to share topic ideas and help shape events as the Southern California SAP network grows. So, if you are from the area or live close by, make sure to integrate into the SAP Community local group and participate in the next events.  

Read Vijayeta blog post here  

SAP Stammtisch in Cyprus recap 

In a recap shared by Vitaly Toraman, the third SAP Stammtisch in Cyprus once again brought together a vibrant group of SAP professionals, representing what Vitaly described as “nearly 100 years of combined SAP experience”. The session focused on S/4HANA transformation and highlighted five essential success factors: strong collaboration between business and IT, clear commitment and decision-making, true process simplification instead of lift-and-shift, structured change management, and early master data cleansing. The discussion underscored how valuable open exchange and local networking are in navigating complex transformation journeys, reinforcing the strength of the SAP Community in Cyprus (click here to join the group). So, if you are in the area, this is a perfect opportunity to connect with this incredible local community group. 

Read Vitaly blog post here 

SAP Inside Track Newtown Square, Philadelphia Summer 2026 

SAP Inside Track Philadelphia returns for its Summer 2026 edition! Planned for mid-June 2026 (9:00 AM - 4:00 PM EDT) at SAP America Headquarters in Newtown Square, PA (or a nearby location, as the organizing team are still planning the venue) this in-person event will once again bring together developers, partners, and SAP experts for a full day of knowledge sharing and networking. The 2026 agenda will focus on the next evolution of Business AI and Cloud ERP, covering topics such as Agentic AI, Joule integrations across SAP solutions, Clean Core strategies with RISE and GROW, and hands-on demos with SAP S/4HANA and Analytics. The organizers are currently building the agenda and invite community members interested in speaking to get involved. If you are in the area or would like to participate, the SAP community in Newtown Square (click here to join the group) is welcoming you. Mark your calendar and stay tuned for more details. 

Read Johna blog post here

entry.title: Re: Your 2026 Roadmap to Getting Started with ABAP AI and ABAP-1
entry.content:
Hi @JanMatthes,  

when @Stephan_Heinberg says, he would love to use it on S/4HANA On-Premise 2025, how can the answer be "it is available on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025"?

I am really interested in a honest explaination here. 

We as developers are sitting on On-Premise systems and want to use your new tooling (sidenote: Because it partly looks very promising!), but can't.

Your (official SAP) reply is to use Private Cloud. How? Do you think we can go to our stakeholders and convince them to sign a RISE contract just for the single reason, that we can use Joule for Developers? That does not happen - nowhere. The customer stays On-Premise and we as developers have to look about alternatives to use AI. This is happening right now. The ABAP community (outside SAP) is hardly discussing about J4D and it's features because hardly someone has access to it "in the real world".  

In addition, I also do not understand your recommendation to use BTP ABAP for side-by-side implementations for older On-Premise systems. Why? Just to be able to use Joule for Developers? There is a clear methodolody by SAP called SAP AEM to decide which way to go, when I want to extend something - and very often the best option is to extend a solution On-Stack using Developer Extensibility with ABAP Cloud in a clean-core-compliant manner. Should I ignore the methodology and architectural aspects just to be able to use Joule for developers during the implementation step on SAP BTP (not even mentoning additional infrastructure costs etc.) ?

I hope I did not offend you personally, but the ABAP community (outside SAP) is significantly annoyed about the lack of availability of Joule for Developers (ABAP AI capabilities) on On-Premise Systems in general - and this needs to be pointed out as much as possible.

It is really sad, that the potentially great features which are developed right now by SAP will never make it into the vast majority of the ABAP developers IDEs - just because of strategical decisions.

Best regards, 

Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Customer Objects Deep Dive in RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard
entry.content:
@GerardoMartinGozalo You might want to post this as a question if this is still not resolved. There is a very low chance to get an answer in a blog comment, especially since this is not directly related to the post subject.

It sounds like you have a central ATC system and are asking if Custom Code Migration App can work with such system. I don't see any indication why it wouldn't and believe the answer is "yes". But it's not something I tried.

The error message seems rather clear though: it says the values in the file don't match the system in the settings. I'm guessing the "selected" system should also be the central system if that's what the file is for. Maybe it's incorrectly set as the actual DEV system, then I'd expect such mismatch to occur.

entry.title: SAP RDP Back Replication of Packaging Fees
entry.content:
Replication Scenario: Packaging Fees to Pricing Conditions and Material Classifications for Spain Plastic Tax

We would like to replicate the following values back to S/4HANA to be printed on invoices:

1) Plastic Tax aggregated on product level

2) kg of non-recylced plastic packaging aggregated on product level

It's not relevant for pricing and should serve only as an information on the invoice. So our assumption is that the Material Classification Mapping is relevant here. The procedure for 2) is well described in the configuration guide. However, we don't find information how to do the mapping for the plastic tax (1). 

Do we:

  • have to adapt Material Classification Mapping in a way so we can map the TotalFee attribute of the replication API to a characteristic value (ATWRT)  of the iDoc? If so, please highlight the iDoc segment structure to be used.
  • Or use the SD mapping to replicate the plastic tax? In that case, please describe the required configuration on S/4 side to use this information not for pricing, but only for invoice printing.

Thank you!

entry.title: Customer credit management issue
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

In the Public Cloud system, customer credit management has been activated. However, the issue is that when a credit limit of 50,000 is maintained in the customer master, a first invoice with a value exceeding 50,000 is still allowed from sales order to billing. If the first or total invoice value exceeds 50,000, the subsequent sales orders are blocked, which is correct.
We want the system to prevent the first invoice or total invoice value from exceeding 50,000.

entry.title: Re: Issues with the new C_S4CFI
entry.content:
Same issue here when taking the system based C_ADBTP_2511 exam last Friday.  Opened a ticket with Learning Support and they replied 3 days later saying that my session is stuck in "running" status.  They say they have contacted the certification team to close my "running" attempt and to credit the attempt back to my account. Then I have to retake the exam...😑

It's been 5 days now and my account still shows that I have an active exam running and I've asked twice for an update with no response from support.  Starting to wonder if all of the support personnel quit...

You should create a support ticket, if you haven't already

entry.title: Re: Your 2026 Roadmap to Getting Started with ABAP AI and ABAP-1
entry.content:
@freilinger ,

The solution is quite simple: customers use OTHER AI code solutions such as CoPilot – and then the money flows to Microsoft, Anthropic and so on... And you know what? If the system really is migrated to the private cloud, developers will NOT reorient themselves because they have become accustomed to the competition. What's more, SAP is waiting... and waiting... and waiting until other solutions get better and better – because they also get more feedback.

I assume that no one at SAP really wants the AI solutions and they just WANT to lose the race – right? Well, if you look at the stock markets, they're doing a good job 🙂
